paper.title: Carbon-aware decentralized dynamic task offloading in MIMO-MEC networks via multi-agent reinforcement learning
paper.abstract: Massive internet of things microservices require integrating renewable energy harvesting into mobile edge computing (MEC) for sustainable eScience infrastructures. Spatiotemporal mismatches between stochastic task arrivals and intermittent green energy along with complex inter-user interference in multi-antenna (MIMO) uplinks complicate real-time resource management. Traditional centralized optimization and off-policy reinforcement learning struggle with scalability and signaling overhead in den

memory_insight.before_after_comparison: CADDTO-PPO reduces memory usage by 30% compared to baseline models

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing decentralized execution with parameter sharing (DEPS) can significantly reduce system latency and energy consumption.
technical_details: Decentralized Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (DEC-POMDP)
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop platforms due to its lightweight inference complexity (O(1)) and efficient memory usage.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
